anxiety: TECHNIQUE: 4-7-8 Breathing. Inhale quietly through nose for 4s, hold for 7s, exhale completely through mouth with a whoosh for 8s.
TECHNIQUE: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding. State 5 things you see, 4 you feel, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste.
WISDOM (Marcus Aurelius): 'Today I escaped anxiety. Or no, I discarded it, because it was within me, in my own perceptions—not outside.'
fear: TECHNIQUE: Fear-setting (Seneca's Premeditatio Malorum). Define exactly the worst-case scenario, how you'd prevent it, and how you'd repair it if it happened.
WISDOM (Seneca): 'We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.'
MANIFESTATION: 'I must not fear. I will face my fear. I will permit it to pass over me and through me.'
focus: TECHNIQUE: Pomodoro 25/5 rhythm. Complete dedication to a single micro-task for 25 minutes. No context switching.
WISDOM (Epictetus): 'If you wish to be an extraordinary person, you cannot do what ordinary people do.'
MANIFESTATION: 'Friction dissolves in the presence of my absolute focus.'
lost: TECHNIQUE: The 10-Minute Brain Dump. Write continuously for 10 minutes without stopping. Don't edit. Let the chaos out onto paper so the mind can rest.
WISDOM (Carl Jung): 'Who looks outside, dreams; who looks inside, awakes.'
WISDOM (Rumi): 'The wound is the place where the Light enters you.'
manifesting: TECHNIQUE: The 'As If' Principle. Act, speak, and make decisions as if the reality you want is already your present reality.
WISDOM (Neville Goddard): 'Assumption, though false, if persisted in, will harden into fact.'
MANIFESTATION: 'I do not chase. I attract. What belongs to me will simply find me.'
stress: TECHNIQUE: Progressive Muscle Relaxation. Tense your toes for 5 seconds, then release. Move up your body muscle by muscle until you reach your face.
WISDOM (Lao Tzu): 'Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.'
TECHNIQUE: Box Breathing. Inhale 4s, hold 4s, exhale 4s, hold empty 4s. Repeat 4 times to reset the parasympathetic nervous system.
sadness: TECHNIQUE: Radical Acceptance. Acknowledge the pain without trying to fight it or push it away. Say 'This is what is happening right now, and it is okay.'
WISDOM (Kahlil Gibran): 'The deeper that sorrow carves into your being, the more joy you can contain.'
TECHNIQUE: Compassionate Witness. Imagine you are a deeply caring, wise friend sitting beside yourself. What gentle words would they say to you right now?
